[[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]<!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]<!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 9 ([[842]]): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * [[Shōwa]] 13 ([[846]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]<!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]<!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]<!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]<!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.

In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.

Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 9 ([[842]]): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * [[Shōwa]] 13 ([[846]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 9 ([[842]]): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * [[Shōwa]] 13 ([[846]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.

{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.

{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.

{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:qq]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.

{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />

December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] == Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]

=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].

{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] Nakano]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:11]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Prince Nakano]][[Category:11]] <!--Prince Nakano-->[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:kofun stubs]] 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.
