{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] {{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />

=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].

{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January[[Category:Prince Nakano]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:11]][[sn:Prince == Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|12=draft}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January
<!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=draft}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January
<!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January
<!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 9 ([[842]]): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * [[Shōwa]] 13 ([[846]]): January
<!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January
<!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January
<!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January
<!--Prince Nakano--> 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略|12=draft}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=draft}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.

December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=draft}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 9 ([[842]]): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * [[Shōwa]] 13 ([[846]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略|12=draft}}』</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|12=draft}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=draft}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 9 ([[842]]): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * [[Shōwa]] 13 ([[846]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略|12=draft}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=draft}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009. <!--Prince Nakano--> <!--Prince Nakano-->

During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|12=draft}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=draft}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 9 ([[842]]): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * [[Shōwa]] 13 ([[846]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January <!--Prince Nakano--> 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略|12=draft}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=draft}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009. <!--Prince Nakano--> <!--Prince Nakano-->

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | Rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court Rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|12=draft|12=draft}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=draft|12=draft}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 9 ([[842]]): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * [[Shōwa]] 13 ([[846]]): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略|12=draft|12=draft}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=draft|12=draft}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.
{{draft categories|
}}

revision.comment: 33 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:仲野親王 up to rev 98158195
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇親 | 名 = 仲野親王 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[奈良時代]]末期 - [[平安時代]]初期 | 生誕 = [[延暦]]11年（[[792年]]） | 薨去 = [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年[[1月17日 (旧暦)|正月17日]]（[[867年]][[2月25日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[山城国]][[葛野郡]][[垂箕山古墳|高畠墓]] | 官位 = [[品位 (位階)|二品]][[大宰帥]]、[[贈位|贈]][[一品親王|一品]][[太政大臣]] | 父母 = 父：[[桓武天皇]]、母：[[藤原河子]] | 兄弟 = [[平城天皇]]、[[朝原内親王]]、[[長岡岡成]]、[[伊予親王]]、[[良岑安世]]、[[嵯峨天皇]]、[[淳和天皇]]、[[葛原親王]]、[[大宅内親王]]、[[高津内親王]]、[[万多親王]]、[[高志内親王]]、[[明日香親王]]、'''仲野親王'''、[[佐味親王]]、[[坂本親王]]、[[賀陽親王]]、[[布勢内親王]]、[[葛井親王]]、[[安勅内親王]]、[[賀楽内親王]]、[[菅原内親王]]、[[甘南美内親王]]、[[伊都内親王]]、[[桓武天皇#后妃・皇子女|他]] | 妻 = [[菅野氏]]、[[当宗氏]] | 子 = [[茂世王]]、[[輔世王]]、[[季世王]]、[[秀世王]]、[[平房世]]、[[当世王]]、[[基世王]]、[[潔世王]]、[[平実世]]、[[十世王]]、[[在世王]]、[[康世王]]、[[胤世王]]、[[平利世]]、[[平惟世]]、[[宜子女王]]、[[班子女王]] | 特記事項 = }} '''仲野親王'''（なかのしんのう）は、[[桓武天皇]]の第12皇子。[[宇多天皇]]の外祖父。[[官位]]は[[二品]]･[[大宰帥]]、[[贈位|贈]][[一品親王|一品]]・[[太政大臣]]。
== 経歴 == [[延暦]]24年（[[805年]]）8月に[[安芸国]][[賀茂郡 (広島県)|賀茂郡]]地50町を、12月に[[河内国]][[交野郡]]白田2町を父の桓武天皇から賜った。
[[嵯峨天皇|嵯峨朝]]の[[弘仁]]5年（[[814年]]）[[品位 (位階)|四品]]に叙せられた。[[淳和天皇|淳和]]朝では[[天長]]3年（[[826年]]）[[上総国#国司|上総太守]]、天長4年（[[827年]]）[[中務省|中務卿]]、天長7年（[[830年]]）[[大宰帥]]に任じられ、天長10年（[[833年]]）正月三品に進んだ。
[[仁明天皇|仁明朝]]に入ると、[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）上総太守、承和9年（[[842年]]）[[弾正尹]]、承和13年（[[846年]]）[[上野国#国司|上野太守]]を経て、同14年（[[847年]]）[[二品]]に至った。[[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]）[[文徳天皇]]の[[即位]]後まもなく[[式部省|式部卿]]に任じられると、[[仁寿]]3年（[[853年]]）[[常陸太守]]、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）上総太守を兼ね、貞観5年（[[863年]]）大宰帥に遷った。晩年、[[輦車]]による宮中の出入り<ref name="a" />と、禁野外での遊猟やそのための鷹と鶴の飼養が許されている<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観3年3月23日条,貞観8年11月29日条</ref>。
貞観9年（[[867年]]）正月17日に[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]した。[[享年]]76。薨後20年を経た[[仁和]]3年（[[887年]]）[[光孝天皇]]の[[女御]]であった娘･[[班子女王]]所生の定省親王が[[即位]]（[[宇多天皇]]）したことにより、天皇の[[外戚|外祖父]]として[[一品親王|一品]][[太政大臣]]が追贈されている。
[[Image:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[垂箕山古墳|高畠墓]]（[[京都市]][[右京区]]）]] ==陵墓== [[山城国]][[葛野郡]]の'''高畠墓'''に葬られた。同墓は『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]に近墓として見える。[[京都市]][[右京区]]太秦垂箕山町に所在する現墓は[[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）に治定されたが、学術上は「[[垂箕山古墳]]」と呼ばれる6世紀後半築造の[[前方後円墳]]であって時代が合わず、あるいはこの地の旧村名「中野村」の由来を親王墓に付会した解釈とも考えられる。ただし、現在のところ他に有力な遺称地などは知られていない。
== 人物 == 幼い頃からわきまえがあって賢く、心が広くゆったりした性格であった<ref name="a">『[[日本三代実録]]』貞観9年正月17日条（薨伝）</ref>。
[[寿詞]]と[[宣命]]を宣読することに優れ、その音儀詞語は模範とされたが、当時の[[皇族]]でその作法に通じていることは非常に珍しいことであった。これは[[左大臣]][[藤原緒嗣]]より伝えられたもので、のちに[[清和天皇]]の命により、当時[[参議]]であった[[藤原基経]]や[[大江音人]]らが仲野親王の住む六条第に赴いて、その音詞曲折を学んだという<ref name="a" />。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[六国史]]』による。 *[[延暦]]24年（[[805年]]） 8月16日：賜[[安芸国]][[賀茂郡 (広島県)|賀茂郡]]地50町。12月20日：賜[[河内国]][[交野郡]]白田2町 *[[弘仁]]5年（[[814年]]） 正月7日：四品 *[[天長]]3年（[[826年]]） 9月：[[上総国#国司|上総太守]]<ref>『[[帝王編年記]]』</ref> *天長4年（[[827年]]） 6月9日：[[中務省|中務卿]] *天長7年（[[830年]]） 8月4日：[[大宰帥]] *天長10年（[[833年]]） 正月7日：三品 *[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]） 正月13日：上総太守 *承和9年（[[842年]]） 11月8日：[[弾正尹]] *承和13年（[[846年]]） 正月13日：[[上野国#国司|上野太守]] *承和14年（[[847年]]） 正月7日：[[二品]] *[[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]） 5月17日：[[式部省|式部卿]] *[[仁寿]]3年（[[853年]]） 日付不詳：兼[[常陸太守]] *[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]） 正月13日：兼上総太守 *貞観5年（[[863年]]） 2月10日：大宰帥 *貞観9年（[[867年]]） 正月17日：[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]（二品） *[[仁和]]3年（[[887年]]） 閏11月15日：[[贈位|贈]][[一品親王|一品]]<ref>『日本紀略』</ref>
== 系譜 == 『[[本朝皇胤紹運録]]』による。 *父：[[桓武天皇]] *母：[[藤原河子]] - [[藤原大継]]の娘 *生母不詳の子女 **男子：[[茂世王]] **男子：[[輔世王]]（? - 879年） **男子：季世王 **男子：[[秀世王]] **男子：[[平房世]]（? - 883年） **四男：[[当世王]]（? - 855年） **男子：[[基世王]] **八男：[[潔世王]]（820年 - 882年） **男子：[[平実世]] **男子：[[十世王]]（833年 - 916年） **男子：[[在世王]] **男子：康世王 **男子：[[平利世]] **男子：[[平惟世]] **男子：胤世王<ref>『日本三代実録』仁和元年9月21日条</ref>
*妻：[[菅野氏]] **女子：[[宜子女王]] - [[斎宮]]
*妻：[[当宗氏]] **女子：[[班子女王]]（833年 - 900年） - [[光孝天皇]][[女御]]、のち[[皇太后]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[森田悌]] 『日本後紀』（上中下巻）[[講談社学術文庫]]、2006年 *森田悌 『続日本後紀』（上下巻）講談社学術文庫、2010年 *[[武田祐吉]]、[[佐藤謙三]]訳 『読み下し 日本三代実録 上巻』[[戎光祥出版]]、2009年
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかのしんのう}} [[Category:日本の親王]] [[Category:奈良時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:平安時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:桓武天皇の子女]] [[Category:9世紀日本の皇族]] [[Category:792年生]] [[Category:867年没]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇親 | 名 = 仲野親王 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[奈良時代]]末期 - [[平安時代]]初期 | 生誕 = [[延暦]]11年（[[792年]]） | 薨去 = [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年[[1月17日 (旧暦)|正月17日]]（[[867年]][[2月25日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[山城国]][[葛野郡]][[垂箕山古墳|高畠墓]] | 官位 = [[品位 (位階)|二品]][[大宰帥]]、[[贈位|贈]][[一品親王|一品]][[太政大臣]] | 父母 = 父：[[桓武天皇]]、母：[[藤原河子]] | 兄弟 = [[平城天皇]]、[[朝原内親王]]、[[長岡岡成]]、[[伊予親王]]、[[良岑安世]]、[[嵯峨天皇]]、[[淳和天皇]]、[[葛原親王]]、[[大宅内親王]]、[[高津内親王]]、[[万多親王]]、[[高志内親王]]、[[明日香親王]]、'''仲野親王'''、[[佐味親王]]、[[坂本親王]]、[[賀陽親王]]、[[布勢内親王]]、[[葛井親王]]、[[安勅内親王]]、[[賀楽内親王]]、[[菅原内親王]]、[[甘南美内親王]]、[[伊都内親王]]、[[桓武天皇#后妃・皇子女|他]] | 妻 = [[菅野氏]]、[[当宗氏]] | 子 = [[茂世王]]、[[輔世王]]、[[季世王]]、[[秀世王]]、[[平房世]]、[[当世王]]、[[基世王]]、[[潔世王]]、[[平実世]]、[[十世王]]、[[在世王]]、[[康世王]]、[[胤世王]]、[[平利世]]、[[平惟世]]、[[宜子女王]]、[[班子女王]] | 特記事項 = }} '''仲野親王'''（なかのしんのう）は、[[桓武天皇]]の第12皇子。[[宇多天皇]]の外祖父。[[官位]]は[[二品]]･[[大宰帥]]、[[贈位|贈]][[一品親王|一品]]・[[太政大臣]]。
== 経歴 == [[延暦]]24年（[[805年]]）8月に[[安芸国]][[賀茂郡 (広島県)|賀茂郡]]地50町を、12月に[[河内国]][[交野郡]]白田2町を父の桓武天皇から賜った。
[[嵯峨天皇|嵯峨朝]]の[[弘仁]]5年（[[814年]]）[[品位 (位階)|四品]]に叙せられた。[[淳和天皇|淳和]]朝では[[天長]]3年（[[826年]]）[[上総国#国司|上総太守]]、天長4年（[[827年]]）[[中務省|中務卿]]、天長7年（[[830年]]）[[大宰帥]]に任じられ、天長10年（[[833年]]）正月三品に進んだ。
[[仁明天皇|仁明朝]]に入ると、[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）上総太守、承和9年（[[842年]]）[[弾正尹]]、承和13年（[[846年]]）[[上野国#国司|上野太守]]を経て、同14年（[[847年]]）[[二品]]に至った。[[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]）[[文徳天皇]]の[[即位]]後まもなく[[式部省|式部卿]]に任じられると、[[仁寿]]3年（[[853年]]）[[常陸太守]]、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）上総太守を兼ね、貞観5年（[[863年]]）大宰帥に遷った。晩年、[[輦車]]による宮中の出入り<ref name="a" />と、禁野外での遊猟やそのための鷹と鶴の飼養が許されている<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観3年3月23日条,貞観8年11月29日条</ref>。
貞観9年（[[867年]]）正月17日に[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]した。[[享年]]76。薨後20年を経た[[仁和]]3年（[[887年]]）[[光孝天皇]]の[[女御]]であった娘･[[班子女王]]所生の定省親王が[[即位]]（[[宇多天皇]]）したことにより、天皇の[[外戚|外祖父]]として[[一品親王|一品]][[太政大臣]]が追贈されている。
[[Image:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[垂箕山古墳|高畠墓]]（[[京都市]][[右京区]]）]] ==陵墓== [[山城国]][[葛野郡]]の'''高畠墓'''に葬られた。同墓は『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]に近墓として見える。[[京都市]][[右京区]]太秦垂箕山町に所在する現墓は[[1875年]]（[[明治]]8年）に治定されたが、学術上は「[[垂箕山古墳]]」と呼ばれる6世紀後半築造の[[前方後円墳]]であって時代が合わず、あるいはこの地の旧村名「中野村」の由来を親王墓に付会した解釈とも考えられる。ただし、現在のところ他に有力な遺称地などは知られていない。
== 人物 == 幼い頃からわきまえがあって賢く、心が広くゆったりした性格であった<ref name="a">『[[日本三代実録]]』貞観9年正月17日条（薨伝）</ref>。
[[寿詞]]と[[宣命]]を宣読することに優れ、その音儀詞語は模範とされたが、当時の[[皇族]]でその作法に通じていることは非常に珍しいことであった。これは[[左大臣]][[藤原緒嗣]]より伝えられたもので、のちに[[清和天皇]]の命により、当時[[参議]]であった[[藤原基経]]や[[大江音人]]らが仲野親王の住む六条第に赴いて、その音詞曲折を学んだという<ref name="a" />。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[六国史]]』による。 *[[延暦]]24年（[[805年]]） 8月16日：賜[[安芸国]][[賀茂郡 (広島県)|賀茂郡]]地50町。12月20日：賜[[河内国]][[交野郡]]白田2町 *[[弘仁]]5年（[[814年]]） 正月7日：四品 *[[天長]]3年（[[826年]]） 9月：[[上総国#国司|上総太守]]<ref>『[[帝王編年記]]』</ref> *天長4年（[[827年]]） 6月9日：[[中務省|中務卿]] *天長7年（[[830年]]） 8月4日：[[大宰帥]] *天長10年（[[833年]]） 正月7日：三品 *[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]） 正月13日：上総太守 *承和9年（[[842年]]） 11月8日：[[弾正尹]] *承和13年（[[846年]]） 正月13日：[[上野国#国司|上野太守]] *承和14年（[[847年]]） 正月7日：[[二品]] *[[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]） 5月17日：[[式部省|式部卿]] *[[仁寿]]3年（[[853年]]） 日付不詳：兼[[常陸太守]] *[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]） 正月13日：兼上総太守 *貞観5年（[[863年]]） 2月10日：大宰帥 *貞観9年（[[867年]]） 正月17日：[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]（二品） *[[仁和]]3年（[[887年]]） 閏11月15日：[[贈位|贈]][[一品親王|一品]]<ref>『日本紀略』</ref>
== 系譜 == 『[[本朝皇胤紹運録]]』による。 *父：[[桓武天皇]] *母：[[藤原河子]] - [[藤原大継]]の娘 *生母不詳の子女 **男子：[[茂世王]] **男子：[[輔世王]]（? - 879年） **男子：季世王 **男子：[[秀世王]] **男子：[[平房世]]（? - 883年） **四男：[[当世王]]（? - 855年） **男子：[[基世王]] **八男：[[潔世王]]（820年 - 882年） **男子：[[平実世]] **男子：[[十世王]]（833年 - 916年） **男子：[[在世王]] **男子：康世王 **男子：[[平利世]] **男子：[[平惟世]] **男子：胤世王<ref>『日本三代実録』仁和元年9月21日条</ref>
*妻：[[菅野氏]] **女子：[[宜子女王]] - [[斎宮]]
*妻：[[当宗氏]] **女子：[[班子女王]]（833年 - 900年） - [[光孝天皇]][[女御]]、のち[[皇太后]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[森田悌]] 『日本後紀』（上中下巻）[[講談社学術文庫]]、2006年 *森田悌 『続日本後紀』（上下巻）講談社学術文庫、2010年 *[[武田祐吉]]、[[佐藤謙三]]訳 『読み下し 日本三代実録 上巻』[[戎光祥出版]]、2009年

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | Rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court Rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 9 ([[842]]): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * [[Shōwa]] 13 ([[846]]): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.

{{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Enryaku]] 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Yamashiro Province]], [[Kadono District]] | Rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sōchi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] [[Daijō-daijin]] | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], [[Emperor Saga]], [[Emperor Junna]], [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Princess Noriko]], [[Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of [[Emperor Uda]]. He held the court Rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]].
== Biography ==
In [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]], in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]], in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of [[Emperor Saga]], he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[Shihin]]) in [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]). Under [[Emperor Junna]], he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Tenchō]] 3, [[826]]), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ([[Tenchō]] 4, [[827]]), and [[Dazai no Sōchi]] ([[Tenchō]] 7, [[830]]). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, [[838]]), [[Danjo no In]] ([[Shōwa]] 9, [[842]]), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ([[Shōwa]] 13, [[846]]). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]). Under [[Emperor Montoku]], he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]). In [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]). In [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sōchi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on [[Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ([[February 25]], [[867]]), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later [[Emperor Uda]]) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and [[Daijō-daijin]] as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], [[Kyoto]])]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], [[Yamashiro Province]]. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryoryo]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in [[1875]] during the [[Meiji era]] but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ([[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from [[Fujiwara no Otsugu]], was later sought by [[Emperor Seiwa]], who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Six National Histories|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}} unless otherwise noted. * [[Enryaku]] 24 ([[805]]): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[Kamo District]], [[Aki Province]]. December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 9 ([[842]]): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * [[Shōwa]] 13 ([[846]]): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=draft}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] – [[Saigū]]
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[Princess Hanako]] ([[833]]–[[900]]) – [[Emperor Kōkō]]'s consort; later [[Kōtaigō]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[Tei Morita]], *Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-3), [[Kōdansha Academic Library]], 2006. * Tei Morita, *Zoku Nihon Kōki* (Volumes 1-2), Kōdansha Academic Library, 2010. * [[Yukichi Takeda]], *Yomikudashi Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (Upper Volume)*, [[Ebisukōshō Publishing]], 2009.
{{translated page|ja|仲野親王|version=98158195|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Prince Nakano}}

}}

December 20: Granted 2 chō in [[Shiroda]], [[Katano District]], [[Kawachi Province]]. * [[Kōnin]] 5 ([[814]]): January 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * [[Tenchō]] 3 ([[826]]): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』</ref> * [[Tenchō]] 4 ([[827]]): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * [[Tenchō]] 7 ([[830]]): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Tenchō]] 10 ([[833]]): January 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ([[838]]): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 9 ([[842]]): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * [[Shōwa]] 13 ([[846]]): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * [[Shōwa]] 14 ([[847]]): January 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * [[Kajō]] 3 ([[850]]): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * [[Ninju]] 3 ([[853]]): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 3 ([[861]]): January 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * [[Jōgan]] 5 ([[863]]): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sōchi]]. * [[Jōgan]] 9 ([[867]]): January 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * [[Ninna]] 3 ([[887]]): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to [[Ippon]].<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』</ref>

[[sn:Prince Nakano]] [[ja:仲野親王]] [[en:Prince Nakano]]
