{{ill|Sugawara-naishinnō|WD=Q15839374|1=Sugawara-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Yoshimine no Yasuyo|WD=Q11614402|1=Yoshimine no Yasuyo}}; {{ill|Ōyake-naishinnō|WD=Q11434304|1=Ōyake-naishinnō}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|WD=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}, {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, [[Kadono District]] | Rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sochi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}} | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|WD=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|WD=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Noriko Senge|Princess Noriko]], [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}. He held the court Rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sochi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}}.
== Biography ==
In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 24 ({{ill|805|en|805|WD=Q30389|lt=805|1=805}}), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[:en:Kamo District|Kamo District]], {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|WD=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[:en:Shihin|Shihin]]) in [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|WD=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3, {{ill|826|en|826|WD=Q30376|lt=826|1=826}}), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 4, {{ill|827|en|827|WD=Q30352|lt=827|1=827}}), and [[Dazai no Sochi]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 7, {{ill|830|en|830|WD=Q30456|lt=830|1=830}}). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 10 ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[:en:Emperor Nimmyō|Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, {{ill|838|en|838|WD=Q23838|lt=838|1=838}}), [[Danjo no In]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9, {{ill|842|en|842|WD=Q24730|lt=842|1=842}}), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 13, {{ill|846|en|846|WD=Q30454|lt=846|1=846}}). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 14 ({{ill|847|en|847|WD=Q30354|lt=847|1=847}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku|1=Emperor Montoku}}, he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in {{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|WD=Q1193892|lt=Kajō|1=Kajō}} 3 ({{ill|850|en|850|WD=Q30364|lt=850|1=850}}). In {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|853|en|853|WD=Q30367|lt=853|1=853}}), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 3 ({{ill|861|en|861|WD=Q23913|lt=861|1=861}}). In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863|1=863}}), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sochi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|WD=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}, {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna|1=Ninna}} 3 ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887|1=887}}), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}} as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}})]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryō-ryō]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Junior Fourth Rank|Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[:en:Imperial Family|Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from {{ill|Fujiwara no Otsugu|en|Fujiwara no Otsugu|WD=Q3090651|lt=Fujiwara no Otsugu|1=Fujiwara no Otsugu}}, was later sought by {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa|1=Emperor Seiwa}}, who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} unless otherwise noted. * {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 24 ({{ill|805|en|805|WD=Q30389|lt=805|1=805}}): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[:en:Kamo District|Kamo District]], {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}. December 20: Granted 2 chō in {{ill|Shiroda|en|Shiroda|WD=Q7498901|lt=Shiroda|1=Shiroda}}, [[Katano District]], {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}. * [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}): January 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3 ({{ill|826|en|826|WD=Q30376|lt=826|1=826}}): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiō Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』</ref> * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 4 ({{ill|827|en|827|WD=Q30352|lt=827|1=827}}): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 7 ({{ill|830|en|830|WD=Q30456|lt=830|1=830}}): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sochi]]. * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 10 ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}): January 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ({{ill|838|en|838|WD=Q23838|lt=838|1=838}}): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9 ({{ill|842|en|842|WD=Q24730|lt=842|1=842}}): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 13 ({{ill|846|en|846|WD=Q30454|lt=846|1=846}}): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 14 ({{ill|847|en|847|WD=Q30354|lt=847|1=847}}): January 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * {{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|WD=Q1193892|lt=Kajō|1=Kajō}} 3 ({{ill|850|en|850|WD=Q30364|lt=850|1=850}}): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|853|en|853|WD=Q30367|lt=853|1=853}}): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 3 ({{ill|861|en|861|WD=Q23913|lt=861|1=861}}): January 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863|1=863}}): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sochi]]. * {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}): January 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna|1=Ninna}} 3 ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887|1=887}}): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to {{ill|Ippon|en|Ippon|WD=Q751522|lt=Ippon|1=Ippon}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|1=Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520403}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Noriko Senge|Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] – {{ill|Saigū|en|Saigū|WD=Q123417769|lt=Saigū|1=Saigū}}
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}–{{ill|900|en|900|WD=Q23460|lt=900|1=900}}) – {{ill|Emperor Kōkō|en|Emperor Kōkō|WD=Q440518|lt=Emperor Kōkō|1=Emperor Kōkō}}'s consort; later [[:en:Kōtaigō|Kōtaigō]].

{{ill|Sugawara-naishinnō|WD=Q15839374|1=Sugawara-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Yoshimine no Yasuyo|WD=Q11614402|1=Yoshimine no Yasuyo}}; {{ill|Ōyake-naishinnō|WD=Q11434304|1=Ōyake-naishinnō}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|WD=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}, {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, [[Kadono District]] | Rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sochi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}} | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|WD=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|WD=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Noriko Senge|Princess Noriko]], [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}. He held the court Rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sochi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}}.
== Biography ==
In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 24 ({{ill|805|en|805|WD=Q30389|lt=805|1=805}}), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[:en:Kamo District|Kamo District]], {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|WD=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[:en:Shihin|Shihin]]) in [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|WD=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3, {{ill|826|en|826|WD=Q30376|lt=826|1=826}}), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 4, {{ill|827|en|827|WD=Q30352|lt=827|1=827}}), and [[Dazai no Sochi]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 7, {{ill|830|en|830|WD=Q30456|lt=830|1=830}}). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 10 ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[:en:Emperor Nimmyō|Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, {{ill|838|en|838|WD=Q23838|lt=838|1=838}}), [[Danjo no In]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9, {{ill|842|en|842|WD=Q24730|lt=842|1=842}}), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 13, {{ill|846|en|846|WD=Q30454|lt=846|1=846}}). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 14 ({{ill|847|en|847|WD=Q30354|lt=847|1=847}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku|1=Emperor Montoku}}, he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in {{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|WD=Q1193892|lt=Kajō|1=Kajō}} 3 ({{ill|850|en|850|WD=Q30364|lt=850|1=850}}). In {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|853|en|853|WD=Q30367|lt=853|1=853}}), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 3 ({{ill|861|en|861|WD=Q23913|lt=861|1=861}}). In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863|1=863}}), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sochi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|WD=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}, {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna|1=Ninna}} 3 ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887|1=887}}), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}} as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}})]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryō-ryō]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Junior Fourth Rank|Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[:en:Imperial Family|Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from {{ill|Fujiwara no Otsugu|en|Fujiwara no Otsugu|WD=Q3090651|lt=Fujiwara no Otsugu|1=Fujiwara no Otsugu}}, was later sought by {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa|1=Emperor Seiwa}}, who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />

December 20: Granted 2 chō in {{ill|Shiroda|en|Shiroda|WD=Q7498901|lt=Shiroda|1=Shiroda}}, [[Katano District]], {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}. * [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}): January 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3 ({{ill|826|en|826|WD=Q30376|lt=826|1=826}}): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiō Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』</ref> * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 4 ({{ill|827|en|827|WD=Q30352|lt=827|1=827}}): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * 10 ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}): January 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ({{ill|838|en|838|WD=Q23838|lt=838|1=838}}): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9 ({{ill|842|en|842|WD=Q24730|lt=842|1=842}}): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 14 ({{ill|847|en|847|WD=Q30354|lt=847|1=847}}): January 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * {{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|WD=Q1193892|lt=Kajō|1=Kajō}} 3 ({{ill|850|en|850|WD=Q30364|lt=850|1=850}}): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|853|en|853|WD=Q30367|lt=853|1=853}}): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 3 ({{ill|861|en|861|WD=Q23913|lt=861|1=861}}): January 13: Concurrently served as Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|1=Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520403}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Noriko Senge|Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] – {{ill|Saigū|en|Saigū|WD=Q123417769|lt=Saigū|1=Saigū}}
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}–{{ill|900|en|900|WD=Q23460|lt=900|1=900}}) – {{ill|Emperor Kōkō|en|Emperor Kōkō|WD=Q440518|lt=Emperor Kōkō|1=Emperor Kōkō}}'s consort; later [[:en:Kōtaigō|Kōtaigō]].

== Biography ==
In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 24 ({{ill|805|en|805|WD=Q30389|lt=805|1=805}}), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[:en:Kamo District|Kamo District]], {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|WD=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[:en:Shihin|Shihin]]) in [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|WD=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3, {{ill|826|en|826|WD=Q30376|lt=826|1=826}}), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 4, {{ill|827|en|827|WD=Q30352|lt=827|1=827}}), and [[Dazai no Sochi]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 7, {{ill|830|en|830|WD=Q30456|lt=830|1=830}}). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 10 ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[:en:Emperor Nimmyō|Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, {{ill|838|en|838|WD=Q23838|lt=838|1=838}}), [[Danjo no In]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9, {{ill|842|en|842|WD=Q24730|lt=842|1=842}}), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 13, {{ill|846|en|846|WD=Q30454|lt=846|1=846}}). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 14 ({{ill|847|en|847|WD=Q30354|lt=847|1=847}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku|1=Emperor Montoku}}, he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in {{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|WD=Q1193892|lt=Kajō|1=Kajō}} 3 ({{ill|850|en|850|WD=Q30364|lt=850|1=850}}). In {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|853|en|853|WD=Q30367|lt=853|1=853}}), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 3 ({{ill|861|en|861|WD=Q23913|lt=861|1=861}}). In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863|1=863}}), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sochi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|WD=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}, {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna|1=Ninna}} 3 ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887|1=887}}), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}} as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}})]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryō-ryō]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Junior Fourth Rank|Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[:en:Imperial Family|Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from {{ill|Fujiwara no Otsugu|en|Fujiwara no Otsugu|WD=Q3090651|lt=Fujiwara no Otsugu|1=Fujiwara no Otsugu}}, was later sought by {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa|1=Emperor Seiwa}}, who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} unless otherwise noted. * {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 24 ({{ill|805|en|805|WD=Q30389|lt=805|1=805}}): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[:en:Kamo District|Kamo District]], {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}. December 20: Granted 2 chō in {{ill|Shiroda|en|Shiroda|WD=Q7498901|lt=Shiroda|1=Shiroda}}, [[Katano District]], {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}. * [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}): January 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3 Kazusa Province]]. * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9 ({{ill|842|en|842|WD=Q24730|lt=842|1=842}}): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * ({{ill|850|en|850|WD=Q30364|lt=850|1=850}}): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|853|en|853|WD=Q30367|lt=853|1=853}}): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 3 ({{ill|861|en|861|WD=Q23913|lt=861|1=861}}): January 13: Concurrently served as Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|1=Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520403}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]

{{ill|Sugawara-naishinnō|WD=Q15839374|1=Sugawara-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Yoshimine no Yasuyo|WD=Q11614402|1=Yoshimine no Yasuyo}}; {{ill|Ōyake-naishinnō|WD=Q11434304|1=Ōyake-naishinnō}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|WD=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}, {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, [[Kadono District]] | Rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sochi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}} | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|WD=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|WD=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Noriko Senge|Princess Noriko]], [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}. He held the court Rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sochi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}}.
== Biography ==
In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 24 ({{ill|805|en|805|WD=Q30389|lt=805|1=805}}), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[:en:Kamo District|Kamo District]], {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|WD=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[:en:Shihin|Shihin]]) in [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|WD=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3, {{ill|826|en|826|WD=Q30376|lt=826|1=826}}), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 4, {{ill|827|en|827|WD=Q30352|lt=827|1=827}}), and [[Dazai no Sochi]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 7, {{ill|830|en|830|WD=Q30456|lt=830|1=830}}). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 10 ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[:en:Emperor Nimmyō|Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, {{ill|838|en|838|WD=Q23838|lt=838|1=838}}), [[Danjo no In]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9, {{ill|842|en|842|WD=Q24730|lt=842|1=842}}), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 13, {{ill|846|en|846|WD=Q30454|lt=846|1=846}}). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 14 ({{ill|847|en|847|WD=Q30354|lt=847|1=847}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku|1=Emperor Montoku}}, he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in {{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|WD=Q1193892|lt=Kajō|1=Kajō}} 3 ({{ill|850|en|850|WD=Q30364|lt=850|1=850}}). In {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|853|en|853|WD=Q30367|lt=853|1=853}}), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 3 ({{ill|861|en|861|WD=Q23913|lt=861|1=861}}). In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863|1=863}}), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sochi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|WD=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}, {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna|1=Ninna}} 3 ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887|1=887}}), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}} as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}})]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryō-ryō]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Junior Fourth Rank|Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[:en:Imperial Family|Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from {{ill|Fujiwara no Otsugu|en|Fujiwara no Otsugu|WD=Q3090651|lt=Fujiwara no Otsugu|1=Fujiwara no Otsugu}}, was later sought by {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa|1=Emperor Seiwa}}, who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />
== Official Chronology == The following timeline is based on the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}} unless otherwise noted. * {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 24 ({{ill|805|en|805|WD=Q30389|lt=805|1=805}}): August 16: Granted 50 chō in [[:en:Kamo District|Kamo District]], {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}. December 20: Granted 2 chō in {{ill|Shiroda|en|Shiroda|WD=Q7498901|lt=Shiroda|1=Shiroda}}, [[Katano District]], {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}. * [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}): January 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3 ({{ill|826|en|826|WD=Q30376|lt=826|1=826}}): September: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]].<ref>『{{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiō Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』</ref> * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 4 ({{ill|827|en|827|WD=Q30352|lt=827|1=827}}): June 9: Appointed [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]]. * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 7 ({{ill|830|en|830|WD=Q30456|lt=830|1=830}}): August 4: Appointed [[Dazai no Sochi]]. * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 10 ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}): January 7: Promoted to [[Third Rank]]. * [[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5 ({{ill|838|en|838|WD=Q23838|lt=838|1=838}}): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9 ({{ill|842|en|842|WD=Q24730|lt=842|1=842}}): November 8: Appointed [[Danjo no In]]. * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 13 ({{ill|846|en|846|WD=Q30454|lt=846|1=846}}): January 13: Appointed [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]]. * {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 14 ({{ill|847|en|847|WD=Q30354|lt=847|1=847}}): January 7: Promoted to [[Second Rank]]. * {{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|WD=Q1193892|lt=Kajō|1=Kajō}} 3 ({{ill|850|en|850|WD=Q30364|lt=850|1=850}}): May 17: Appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]]. * {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|853|en|853|WD=Q30367|lt=853|1=853}}): Date unknown: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Hitachi Province]]. * {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 3 ({{ill|861|en|861|WD=Q23913|lt=861|1=861}}): January 13: Concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]]. * {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863|1=863}}): February 10: Appointed [[Dazai no Sochi]]. * {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}): January 17: Passed away (Rank: Second Rank). * {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna|1=Ninna}} 3 ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887|1=887}}): Intercalary 11th month, 15th day: Posthumously promoted to {{ill|Ippon|en|Ippon|WD=Q751522|lt=Ippon|1=Ippon}}.<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|1=Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520403}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Noriko Senge|Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] – {{ill|Saigū|en|Saigū|WD=Q123417769|lt=Saigū|1=Saigū}}
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}–{{ill|900|en|900|WD=Q23460|lt=900|1=900}}) – {{ill|Emperor Kōkō|en|Emperor Kōkō|WD=Q440518|lt=Emperor Kōkō|1=Emperor Kōkō}}'s consort; later [[:en:Kōtaigō|Kōtaigō]].

{{ill|Sugawara-naishinnō|WD=Q15839374|1=Sugawara-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Yoshimine no Yasuyo|WD=Q11614402|1=Yoshimine no Yasuyo}}; {{ill|Ōyake-naishinnō|WD=Q11434304|1=Ōyake-naishinnō}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|WD=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}, {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, [[Kadono District]] | Rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sochi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}} | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|WD=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|WD=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Noriko Senge|Princess Noriko]], [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}. He held the court Rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sochi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}}.
== Biography ==
In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 24 ({{ill|805|en|805|WD=Q30389|lt=805|1=805}}), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[:en:Kamo District|Kamo District]], {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|WD=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[:en:Shihin|Shihin]]) in [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|WD=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3, {{ill|826|en|826|WD=Q30376|lt=826|1=826}}), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 4, {{ill|827|en|827|WD=Q30352|lt=827|1=827}}), and [[Dazai no Sochi]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 7, {{ill|830|en|830|WD=Q30456|lt=830|1=830}}). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 10 ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[:en:Emperor Nimmyō|Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, {{ill|838|en|838|WD=Q23838|lt=838|1=838}}), [[Danjo no In]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9, {{ill|842|en|842|WD=Q24730|lt=842|1=842}}), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 13, {{ill|846|en|846|WD=Q30454|lt=846|1=846}}). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 14 ({{ill|847|en|847|WD=Q30354|lt=847|1=847}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku|1=Emperor Montoku}}, he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in {{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|WD=Q1193892|lt=Kajō|1=Kajō}} 3 ({{ill|850|en|850|WD=Q30364|lt=850|1=850}}). In {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|853|en|853|WD=Q30367|lt=853|1=853}}), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 3 ({{ill|861|en|861|WD=Q23913|lt=861|1=861}}). In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863|1=863}}), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sochi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|WD=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}, {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna|1=Ninna}} 3 ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887|1=887}}), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}} as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}})]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryō-ryō]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Junior Fourth Rank|Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[:en:Imperial Family|Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from {{ill|Fujiwara no Otsugu|en|Fujiwara no Otsugu|WD=Q3090651|lt=Fujiwara no Otsugu|1=Fujiwara no Otsugu}}, was later sought by {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa|1=Emperor Seiwa}}, who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />

December 20: Granted 2 chō in {{ill|Shiroda|en|Shiroda|WD=Q7498901|lt=Shiroda|1=Shiroda}}, [[Katano District]], {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}. * [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}): January 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3 Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|1=Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520403}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Noriko Senge|Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] – {{ill|Saigū|en|Saigū|WD=Q123417769|lt=Saigū|1=Saigū}}
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}–{{ill|900|en|900|WD=Q23460|lt=900|1=900}}) – {{ill|Emperor Kōkō|en|Emperor Kōkō|WD=Q440518|lt=Emperor Kōkō|1=Emperor Kōkō}}'s consort; later [[:en:Kōtaigō|Kōtaigō]].

{{ill|Kōzu-naishinnō|WD=Q11671787|1=Kōzu-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Manta-shinnō|WD=Q11353795|1=Manta-shinnō}}; {{ill|Sakamoto-shinnō|WD=Q18193371|1=Sakamoto-shinnō}}; {{ill|Sami-shinnō|WD=Q13292202|1=Sami-shinnō}}; {{ill|Sugawara-naishinnō|WD=Q15839374|1=Sugawara-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Yoshimine no Yasuyo|WD=Q11614402|1=Yoshimine no Yasuyo}}; {{ill|Ōyake-naishinnō|WD=Q11434304|1=Ōyake-naishinnō}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Imperial Family | name = Prince Nakano (仲野親王) | image = | caption = | period = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 11 ([[792]]) | death_date = [[Jōgan (Japan)|Jōgan]] 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|WD=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}, {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = [[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}, [[Kadono District]] | Rank = [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) [[Dazai no Sochi]], [[Posthumous Promotions|Posthumously promoted]] to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}} | parents = Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]]; Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] | siblings = [[Emperor Heizei]], [[Princess Asahara]], [[Nagaoka Okanari]], [[Prince Iyo]], [[Yoshimine no Yasuyo]], {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|WD=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|WD=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, [[Prince Kazurahara]], [[Princess Ōyake]], [[Princess Takatsu]], [[Prince Manta]], [[Princess Koshi]], [[Prince Asuka]], Prince Nakano, [[Prince Sami]], [[Prince Sakamoto]], [[Prince Kaya]], [[Princess Fuse]], [[Prince Katsuragi]], [[Princess Anjiki]], [[Princess Garaku]], [[Princess Sugawara]], [[Princess Kanami]], [[Princess Ito]], [[Emperor Kanmu#Consorts and Issue|others]] | spouse = [[Sugano clan]], [[Tōsō clan]] | children = [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]], [[Noriko Senge|Princess Noriko]], [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] | notable_works = }} Prince Nakano (仲野親王) was the twelfth son of [[Emperor Kanmu]] and the maternal grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}. He held the court Rank of [[Second Rank]] ([[Nihin]]) and served as [[Dazai no Sochi]]. Posthumously, he was promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}}.
== Biography ==
In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 24 ({{ill|805|en|805|WD=Q30389|lt=805|1=805}}), Prince Nakano was granted 50 chō of land in [[:en:Kamo District|Kamo District]], {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}, in August and 2 chō of Shiroda in [[Katano District]], {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, in December by his father, Emperor Kanmu.
During the [[Saga era]] of {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|WD=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, he was elevated to the [[Imperial Rank|Fourth Rank]] ([[:en:Shihin|Shihin]]) in [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|WD=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, he served in several key posts, including [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3, {{ill|826|en|826|WD=Q30376|lt=826|1=826}}), [[Nakatsukasa no Kami]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 4, {{ill|827|en|827|WD=Q30352|lt=827|1=827}}), and [[Dazai no Sochi]] ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 7, {{ill|830|en|830|WD=Q30456|lt=830|1=830}}). He was promoted to the [[Imperial Rank|Third Rank]] in {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 10 ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}).
During the [[Nimmyō era]] of [[:en:Emperor Nimmyō|Emperor Nimmyō]], Prince Nakano held posts such as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] ([[Shōwa (Japan)|Shōwa]] 5, {{ill|838|en|838|WD=Q23838|lt=838|1=838}}), [[Danjo no In]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 9, {{ill|842|en|842|WD=Q24730|lt=842|1=842}}), and [[Governor of Kōzuke Province]] ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 13, {{ill|846|en|846|WD=Q30454|lt=846|1=846}}). He attained the [[Imperial Rank|Second Rank]] in {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|WD=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 14 ({{ill|847|en|847|WD=Q30354|lt=847|1=847}}). Under {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|WD=Q357225|lt=Emperor Montoku|1=Emperor Montoku}}, he was appointed [[Shikibu no Kami]] shortly after Montoku's enthronement in {{ill|Kajō|en|Kajō|WD=Q1193892|lt=Kajō|1=Kajō}} 3 ({{ill|850|en|850|WD=Q30364|lt=850|1=850}}). In {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|853|en|853|WD=Q30367|lt=853|1=853}}), he became [[Governor of Hitachi Province]], and later, he concurrently served as [[Governor of Kazusa Province]] in {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 3 ({{ill|861|en|861|WD=Q23913|lt=861|1=861}}). In {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 5 ({{ill|863|en|863|WD=Q25052|lt=863|1=863}}), he was reassigned to [[Dazai no Sochi]].
Prince Nakano passed away on {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9, [[1st month, 17th day]] ({{ill|February 25|en|February 25|WD=Q2356|lt=February 25|1=February 25}}, {{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}), at the age of 76. Twenty years after his death, in {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna|1=Ninna}} 3 ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887|1=887}}), when his grandson, [[Prince Sadazane]] (later {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}) ascended the throne, Prince Nakano was posthumously promoted to [[Imperial Prince of the First Rank|Ippon]] and {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō-daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}} as the maternal grandfather of the emperor.
[[File:Tomb of Prince Nakano.jpg|260px|thumb|[[Takabata Tomb]] (高畠墓), [[Ukyo Ward, Kyoto|Ukyo-ku]], {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}})]]
== Burial == Prince Nakano was interred at the Takabata Tomb in [[Kadono District]], {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}}. This tomb, mentioned as a "nearby grave" in the [[Engishiki]]'s [[Shoryō-ryō]], is located in what is now [[Taima, Ukyo-ku]], Kyoto. The present tomb was designated in {{ill|1875|en|1875|WD=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} but is academically identified as the "[[Tarumiyama Kofun]]" — a [[keyhole-shaped tumulus]] ({{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}) from the late 6th century, predating Prince Nakano’s era. The connection between the tomb and Prince Nakano may stem from the association with the old village name "Nakano Village," though no definitive evidence supports this claim.
== Personality == Prince Nakano was known from an early age for his wisdom, decorum, and magnanimous personality.<ref name="a">『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』Jōgan 9, 1st month, 17th day (Obituary)</ref>
He excelled in reciting [[Junior Fourth Rank|Jushi]] and [[Senmyō]], and his tone and diction were regarded as exemplary. It was rare for members of the [[:en:Imperial Family|Imperial Family]] to possess such mastery. This knowledge, passed down from {{ill|Fujiwara no Otsugu|en|Fujiwara no Otsugu|WD=Q3090651|lt=Fujiwara no Otsugu|1=Fujiwara no Otsugu}}, was later sought by {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|WD=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa|1=Emperor Seiwa}}, who instructed officials such as [[Fujihara no Mototsune]] and [[Ōe no Oto]] to study this art at Prince Nakano's residence.<ref name="a" />

Province}}. * [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 5 ({{ill|814|en|814|WD=Q23447|lt=814|1=814}}): January 7: Elevated to [[Fourth Rank]]. * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 3 Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}』</ref>
== Ancestry == As recorded in the {{ill|Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|ja|本朝皇胤紹運録|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Kōin Jōunroku|1=Honchō Kōin Shōunroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520403}}: * Father: [[Emperor Kanmu]] * Mother: [[Fujiwara no Kashi]] – Daughter of [[Fujiwara no Ōtsugu]]
=== Children (Mother Unknown) === * Sons: [[Prince Moyō]], [[Prince Sukeyo]], [[Prince Kiseyo]], [[Prince Hideyo]], [[Taira no Fusasada]], [[Prince Tōsō]], [[Prince Motoyo]], [[Prince Kiyosayo]], [[Taira no Sanesada]], [[Prince Jusō]], [[Prince Zaisyō]], [[Prince Kōsō]], [[Prince Tanesada]], [[Taira no Tokisada]], [[Taira no Koreyo]] * Daughter: [[Noriko Senge|Princess Noriko]] (Saigū)
=== With [[Sugano clan]] === * Daughter: [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] – {{ill|Saigū|en|Saigū|WD=Q123417769|lt=Saigū|1=Saigū}}
=== With [[Tōsō clan]] === * Daughter: [[:en:Princess Hanako|Princess Hanako]] ({{ill|833|en|833|WD=Q30377|lt=833|1=833}}–{{ill|900|en|900|WD=Q23460|lt=900|1=900}}) – {{ill|Emperor Kōkō|en|Emperor Kōkō|WD=Q440518|lt=Emperor Kōkō|1=Emperor Kōkō}}'s consort; later [[:en:Kōtaigō|Kōtaigō]].
